title: cordylanthus tenuis subsp. capillaris
intro: Cordylanthus tenuis subsp. capillaris, also known as purple owl's clover, is a rare, perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a type of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that has been traditionally used for its medicinal and spiritual properties. The medicinal properties of Cordylanthus tenuis subsp. capillaris include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions, such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating respiratory problems.
